data.title: Burgerizzr Posts Decline in FY24 Net Profit; Revenue Jumps
data.description:
Shatirah House Restaurant (SASE:6016), d/b/a Burgerizzr, said Sunday that profit for full-year 2024 decreased, while revenue rose year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 8.4 million Saudi riyals, compared with 12.3 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.24 riyal from 0.35 riyal.

The Saudi burger chain operator's revenue was 299.6 million riyals, compared with 281.1 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: US firm Firefly's Blue Ghost moon lander locks in for lunar touchdown
data.description:
Key points:
  • Firefly Aerospace nears lunar touchdown with Blue Ghost lander
  • Moon landing would be company's first, second for a private firm
  • Firefly one of many companies in modern moon race led by US, China

Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost moon lander tightened its lunar orbit on Sunday before its inaugural attempt at an uncrewed touchdown, nearing a pivotal moment for one of a handful of private firms on the frontlines of a global moon race.

The size of a compact car, the four-legged Blue Ghost is carrying 10 scientific payloads and using 21 thrusters to guide toward its scheduled 3:45 a.m. ET (0845 GMT) touchdown near an ancient volcanic vent on Mare Crisium, a large basin in the northeast corner of the moon's Earth-facing side.

Firefly is seeking to be the second private firm to score a soft moon landing. Houston-based Intuitive Machines' Odysseus lander made a lopsided soft touchdown last year. Five nations have made successful soft landings in the past - the then-Soviet Union, the U.S., China, India and, last year, Japan.

Flight controllers at Firefly's Austin, Texas, headquarters sent final commands to Blue Ghost as it lowered its lunar orbit, flying about 238,000 miles (383,000 km) from Earth a month and a half after launching atop a SpaceX rocket from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida.

The moonshot by Firefly, an upstart primarily building rockets, is one of three lunar missions actively in progress. Japan's ispace launched its second lander on the same rocket as Firefly's in January, before Intuitive Machines embarked on its second lunar mission on Wednesday.

Backed by NASA and its flagship Artemis moon program, private companies are playing an outsized role in the modern moon race with the hopes of stimulating a lunar market. Elon Musk's SpaceX and Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin are building landers to put U.S. astronauts on the moon as soon as 2027 for the first time since 1972.

CROWDED MOON RACE

Missions like Firefly's Blue Ghost represent low-budget precursor missions to the moon that will enable research into the lunar environment before the U.S. sends its astronauts there. Firefly has a $101 million NASA contract for the mission.

China, meanwhile, is making swift progress in its own moon efforts, with its robotic Chang'e lunar program and plans to put Chinese astronauts on the moon's surface by 2030. Also eyeing the moon are U.S.-aligned Japan and India, which made its first soft lunar landing in 2023.

Blue Ghost has two navigation cameras to spot hazards on the lunar surface during its final descent and help the spacecraft steer toward an ideal landing spot. The craft's four carbon-composite legs have impact sensors that will trigger Blue Ghost's engine to shutdown upon landing.

Two solar panels on the side and one of the top will power the lander and its research instruments for a 14-day mission on the moon, before the frigid lunar night brings temperatures as low as minus 280 degrees Fahrenheit (minus 173 degrees Celsius).

Two onboard instruments will study the lunar soil and its subsurface temperatures in experiments by Honeybee Robotics, a firm owned by Blue Origin, which is developing its own lunar lander to send humans to the moon for NASA's Artemis program later this decade.

NASA's Langley Research Center has a stereo camera on board to analyze the lunar dirt plumes kicked up by Blue Ghost's landing engine, gathering data to help researchers predict the dusty surface material's dispersal during heavier moon missions in the future.

Research into landing plumes is a prominent technical issue at the center of discussions on how countries such as the U.S. and China will coexist on nearby areas of the moon. Plume behavior is a factor behind the "safety zones" envisioned by NASA's space safety pact, the Artemis Accords, to prevent interference or damage to prospective neighbors on the moon.

data.title: Meyer Burger Secures Further Extension for $40 Million Bridge Facility
data.description:
Meyer Burger Technology secured another extension for the maturity of its bridge facility worth $39.5 million.

The maturity date for the Swiss solar cells and solar modules manufacturer's facility was extended to March 5, according to a March 1 release.

data.title: Twareat Medical Care Lands 12-month Medical Services Contract with Neom
data.description:
Twareat Medical Care (SASE:9627) signed a 12-month medical services contract with Neom, according to a Sunday release.

The memorandum of understanding covers the delivery of health and well-being services to the Neom region in Saudi Arabia.

data.title: Canadian Medical Center Bags SAR31 Million Contract at Neom Urban Development
data.description:
Canadian Medical Center (SASE:9518) signed a 30.8 million-riyal agreement with Saudi Arabia's planned mega urban development Neom for the management and operation of clinics.

Under the one-year contract, the Saudi Arabian medical company will operate medical clinics in residential complexes and offer healthcare services like medical staff, equipment, and emergency services, according to a Sunday release.

The deal is expected to have a positive impact on the group's financials from the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Al-Rajhi Co. for Cooperative Insurance's FY24 Profit, Insurance Revenue Rise
data.description:
Al-Rajhi Co. for Cooperative Insurance (SASE:8230) said Sunday that profit and insurance revenue for full-year 2024 rose year over year.

Net profit after zakat attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 332.3 million Saudi riyals, compared with 328.1 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 3.32 riyals from 3.28 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed multi-line insurance company's insurance revenue was 5.39 billion riyals, compared with 4.24 billion riyals a year ago.

data.title: Al Hammadi Posts Higher FY24 Net Profit; Revenue Drops
data.description:
Al Hammadi Holding (SASE:4007) said Sunday that profit for full-year 2024 gained, while revenue declined year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 338.8 million Saudi riyals, compared with 303.3 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 2.12 riyals from 1.9 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-based integrated healthcare group's revenue was 1.15 billion riyals, compared with 1.18 billion riyals a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board declared an unchanged cash dividend of 0.35 riyal per share for the fourth quarter of 2024, payable on March 16 to shareholders on record Sunday.

data.title: Saudi Fisheries Fully Clears Accumulated Losses
data.description:
Saudi Fisheries (SASE:6050) said Sunday that it fully extinguished its accumulated losses through a capital decrease.

The Saudi Arabian fishing company's capital declined to 67 million Saudi riyals from 400 million riyals via the cancellation of 33,301,396 shares.

data.title: Retal Urban Development Reports Higher FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Retal Urban Development (SASE:4322) said Sunday that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 rose year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 266.1 million Saudi riyals, compared with 202.4 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.53 riyal from 0.4 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed real estate developer's revenue was 2.06 billion riyals, compared with 1.37 billion riyals a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a dividend of 0.11 riyal per share for the second half of 2024, payable May 15 to shareholders on record May 4. It paid 0.16 riyal per share a year ago.

data.title: Armah Sports FY24 Profit, Revenue Surges
data.description:
Armah Sports (SASE:9590) said Sunday that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 climbed year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 37.8 million Saudi riyals, compared with 800,000 riyals earlier. EPS moved to 1.15 riyals from 0.03 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed fitness brand owner's revenue was 177.5 million riyals, compared with 80.1 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: 2P Lands SAR67 Million Contract for Contact Center Development
data.description:
Perfect Presentation for Commercial Services (SASE:7204), d/b/a 2P, was hired to establish and operate a unified call center called voice interactions at National Water Co. for 66.6 million Saudi riyals.

Under the contract, the Saudi Arabia-based IT company will provide services for the development of the contact center premises, personnel hiring, and operations management, among other technical services, according to a Sunday release.

data.title: China's central bank, regulators hold symposium on financial support of private enterprises
data.description:
China's central bank and financial regulators held a symposium with private enterprises and financial institutions, vowing to support high-quality development of private enterprises, the People's Bank of China said on Sunday.

Executives from Eve Group, Geely Holdings, SenseTime , YTO Express and Inner Mongolia Yili Industrial Group attended Friday's symposium, the PBOC said in a statement.

  • SHATIRAH HOUSE RESTAURANT FY SALES 299.6 MILLION RIYALS

data.title: Thob Al Aseel Posts Growth in FY24 Net Profit; Revenue Drops
data.description:
Thob Al Aseel (SASE:4012) said Sunday that profit for full-year 2024 increased, while revenue slipped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 83.9 million Saudi riyals, compared with 76.9 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.21 riyal from 0.19 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-based garment manufacturer's revenue was 522.5 million riyals, compared with 532.2 million riyals a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board declared an unchanged cash dividend of 0.1 riyal per share for the second half of 2024, payable within 15 business days to shareholders on record Monday.

data.title: Al Mohafaza Co. for Education Secures License for School Operations in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Al Mohafaza Co. for Education (SASE:9598) secured a school operations license from Saudi's General Organization for Technical and Vocational Training.

The license will allow the Saudi Arabian school company to conduct training diplomas, qualification programs, and development programs at its Al-Muhafaza Higher Institute for Training facility in Saudi Arabia, according to a Sunday release.

data.title: Uganda reports second Ebola death, a 4-year-old, WHO says
data.description:
A second Ebola patient, a four-year-old child, has died in Uganda, the World Health Organization said, citing the country’s health ministry.

The fatality brings the number of confirmed cases in Uganda to 10.

The East African country declared an outbreak of the highly infectious and often fatal haemorrhagic disease in January after the death of a male nurse at the Mulago National Referral Hospital in the capital Kampala.

The WHO's Uganda office posted late on Saturday on X that the ministry had reported "an additional positive case in Mulago hospital of a four-and-a-half-year-old child, who tragically passed away" on Tuesday.

Mulago is the country's sole national referral hospital for Ebola cases.

The ministry said on February 18 that all eight Ebola patients under care had been discharged but that at least 265 contacts remained under strict quarantine in Kampala and two other cities.

Ebola symptoms include fever, headache and muscle pains. The virus is transmitted through contact with infected bodily fluids and tissue.

data.title: Abdullah Al Othaim Markets to Buy 51% Stake in Ebdaa Al Qasr Marketing
data.description:
Abdullah Al Othaim Markets (SASE:4001) entered into a nonbinding agreement to purchase a 51% stake in food and consumer goods retailer Ebdaa Al Qasr Marketing.

The retail company's 90-day renewable memorandum of understanding is subject to share valuation, due diligence, and regulatory approval, according to a Sunday release.

data.title: Ericsson-led Aduna, Bridge Alliance Partner for Application Programming Interfaces
data.description:
Ericsson's (ERIC-B.ST, ERIC-A.ST) Aduna venture with global telecom companies will collaborate with Bridge Alliance to boost the adoption of Camara-based network application programming interfaces, according to a March 1 release.

Aduna and the mobile alliance in the Asia Pacific, Middle East, and Africa will also team up in support of critical global standardization efforts, the Swedish telecom equipment maker said.

data.title: Alrajhi Takaful FY Profit Rises
data.description:

  • ALRAJHI TAKAFUL FY NET PROFIT ATTRIBUTABLE 332.3 MILLION RIYALS VERSUS 328.1 MILLION RIYALS YEAR AGO

data.title: Saudi Tadawul Group Posts Growth in FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Saudi Tadawul Group (SASE:1111) said Sunday that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 grew year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 621.8 million Saudi riyals, compared with 390.1 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 5.18 riyals from 3.25 riyals.

The Saudi bourse's revenue was 1.45 billion riyals, compared with 1.07 billion riyals a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a dividend of 3.35 riyals per share for the period, with eligibility and distribution dates disclosed later. It paid 2.30 riyals per share a year ago.

data.title: Autoneum Concludes Acquisition of Majority Stake in Chinese Automotive Supplier
data.description:
Autoneum Holding closed the planned purchase of a 70% interest in Chinese automotive supplier Jiangsu Huanyu Group after securing regulatory approvals.

The maker of components for light and commercial vehicles, which operates 13 production facilities, will retain its Chinese name. The Swiss automotive parts manufacturer has the option to buy the remaining 30% of Jiangsu Huanyu in 2028.

data.title: J&J Seeks $9 Billion Settlement to End Unit's 15-year Talc Litigation
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson is looking to secure a $9 billion settlement to end a 15-year lawsuit over claims that the company's talc powder caused cancer.

The proposal was followed after its Red River Talc unit held a pre-bankruptcy vote in 2024, in which 80% of talc claimants voted in favor of the settlement, Bloomberg News reported March 1. The vote would see the payment of $120,000 for each victim.

Other claimants, however, said that the vote was not credible and not handled properly.

data.title: Emaar The Economic City Offsets Accumulated Losses via Capital Reduction
data.description:
Emaar The Economic City (SASE:4220) cut its accumulated losses to 0% via a capital decrease, according to a Feb. 27 release.

The real estate developer canceled 610,073,425 shares, resulting in its capital to decline to 5.23 billion Saudi riyals from 11.33 billion riyals.

data.title: India's Adani Group revives US investment plans, FT reports
data.description:
India's Adani Group has revived plans for major infrastructure investments in the U.S., where the group's founder has been charged with bribery, the Financial Times reported on Sunday.

Since the election of President Donald Trump, the conglomerate has reactivated potential plans to fund projects in sectors such as nuclear power and utilities, as well as an East Coast port, the report said, citing four people close to founder and chair Gautam Adani.

Federal prosecutors in New York unsealed an indictment in November accusing Gautam Adani of bribing Indian officials to persuade them to buy electricity produced by Adani Green Energy .

"We know what we want to do, but we will wait until this (case) resolves," the FT quoted a person close to Adani as saying.

Adani Group has said the charges were "baseless" and that it would seek "all possible legal recourse." It did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment on the FT report.

The group had previously been in talks with U.S. companies on potential partnerships and had looked at petrochemical investments in Texas, the newspaper said.

After Trump's November election win, Gautam Adani said the group planned to invest $10 billion in U.S. energy security and infrastructure projects, creating a potential 15,000 jobs.

Trump has vowed to make it easier for energy companies to drill on federal land and build pipelines.

"Once Trump came in, we have reactivated some plans," the FT said, citing another source it did not name.

The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission asked Indian authorities last month for help in its investigation of Gautam Adani and his nephew Sagar Adani over allegations of securities fraud and a $265-million bribery scheme.

In 2023 the conglomerate was accused by U.S.-based short-seller Hindenburg Research, which disbanded earlier this year, of improper use of offshore tax havens and stock manipulation that sparked a $150 billion rout in shares of the group's companies. Adani denied those allegations.
